title: canthium tavoyanum var. glabrius
intro: Canthium tavoyanum var. glabrius, also known as the Burmese canthium, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia and is a variant of the Canthium tavoyanum plant. It is a shrub or small tree with medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat a variety of health conditions, including reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, and has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of the Burmese canthium can be made into teas, infusions, or decoctions for medicinal use.
